title: Paint and Stain Storage Shelf
summary: This project has you covered for a way to store paint and stain in an organized way.
description: This project has you covered for a way to store paint and stain in an organized way. This easy DIY garage paint storage system will display your paints and stains and keep you organized!

steps.title: Build Top Shelves
steps.body: Attach a Top Front board to a Top Back board using pocket screws in the three pocket holes you drilled. Repeat for the second top shelf. These shelves are designed for stain cans so you can see the front of the can.

steps.title: Build Middle Shelves
steps.body: Attach a Middle Front board to a Middle Back board using pocket screws in the three pocket holes you drilled. Repeat for the second middle shelf. These shelves are designed for quart sized paint cans so you can see the name of the paint on the top of the can.

steps.title: Build Bottom Shelves
steps.body: Attach a Bottom Front board to a Bottom Back board using pocket screws in the three pocket holes you drilled. Repeat for the second bottom shelf. These shelves are designed for gallon sized paint cans so you can see the name of the paint on the top of the can.

steps.title: Attach Side One
steps.body: Lay one of the Sides down and lay out each of the shelves on the board. Arrange with spacing and tilt how you like, but ours were done as follows for the height of the front lip of each shelf. Each shelf was angled about 15 degrees back. Top Shelf 1- 43" from bottom of Side Top Shelf 2- 35" from bottom of Side Middle Shelf 1- 29" from bottom of Side Middle Shelf 2- 22" from bottom of Side Bottom Shelf 1- 13" from bottom of Side Bottom Shelf 2- 4" from bottom of Side Attach with two pocket holes drilled on one side of each shelf.

steps.title: Attach Side Two
steps.body: Line up the second Side with the same distances as Side one and attach with pocket holes on the other side of the shelves.

steps.title: Stain or Paint
steps.body: Bust out one of the stains or paints you're about to store on this project and color it as you'd like. Or leave natural.

steps.title: Enjoy
steps.body: Enjoy your handiwork and tag @CharlestonCrafted on Instagram with a picture!
